source_basis: Claude Skills-style systems use modular instruction/script/resource bundles that are selected as needed.
MCP/tool security research shows descriptor and metadata manipulation can affect planning/tool selection.
Recent delegation reliability reporting shows long workflows need checkpoints and evidence controls.
research_findings: Plan quality must include gate/evidence mapping, not only step sequencing.
Skill/tool selection needs an auditable trigger trace.
Plans need rollback or handoff boundaries for delegated workflows.
applied_upgrades: Add skill_trigger_trace as an output expectation.
Add skill/tool vetting to edge cases.
remaining_risks: Need trial dataset for measuring gap-catching quality.
Need automated planned-gate vs actual-evidence comparison.
sources: Claude Skills coverage: folder-based instructions/scripts/resources
MCP security: tool poisoning, shadowing, rug-pull and descriptor manipulation research
MCP Security Bench: planning, invocation and response-handling attack taxonomy
LLM delegation reliability reporting

source_basis: Delegated LLM workflows can silently corrupt artifacts, so QA must require evidence checkpoints.
Frontend/API workflows need state and contract coverage, not only happy-path snapshots.
research_findings: Missing evidence must block release separately from failed evidence.
Evidence strength should be explicit because generated claims are not proof.
Regression risk must be tied to changed paths and core user flows.
remaining_risks: Evidence scoring remains heuristic until trials calibrate it.
sources: LLM delegation reliability reporting
Frontend/UI state coverage and accessibility testing practice

source_basis: AI-assisted work benefits from fast iteration, but delegated workflows can damage artifacts without oversight.
Lean execution principles support minimal useful increments when scope cuts remain visible.
research_findings: Execution speed needs explicit scope-cut traceability.
Fast path must preserve safety, evidence and rollback gates.
Blockers should become next actions or escalations, not disappear.
applied_upgrades: Add silent scope loss as edge case.
remaining_risks: Could still push too fast before discovery.
Needs Runner behavior that turns blockers into suggested next actions.
sources: LLM delegation reliability reporting
Lean delivery / minimal useful increment practice

source_basis: LLM delegation studies show plausible outputs can contain sparse but severe errors.
Review practice needs separation of spec conformance, implementation quality, severity and evidence.
research_findings: Review needs claim-to-evidence mapping for high-impact outputs.
Severity classification prevents review noise from blocking all progress.
Review should run before memory update to avoid storing bad conclusions.
sources: LLM delegation reliability reporting
LLM evaluation and software review practice

source_basis: MCP security research documents tool poisoning, shadowing and descriptor-level manipulation.
MCP Security Bench evaluates attacks across planning, invocation and response handling.
Skill/plugin mechanisms with executable resources create source-trust concerns.
research_findings: Ultra review must check tool/skill metadata trust, not only output correctness.
Memory/context side effects are safety-relevant.
Findings need prioritization to avoid over-blocking useful progress.
applied_upgrades: Add descriptor poisoning and untrusted skill source edge cases.
remaining_risks: Needs severity model for security vs delivery tradeoffs.
sources: MCP security: tool poisoning, shadowing, rug-pull and descriptor manipulation research
MCPTox benchmark: tool poisoning against real MCP servers
MCP Security Bench: planning, invocation and response-handling attack taxonomy
Claude Skills / executable skill safety coverage

source_basis: Agent memory surveys model memory as write-manage-read loops with contradiction handling and privacy governance.
Experience-following behavior can propagate errors and replay irrelevant old memories.
research_findings: Context mode must manage visible drop lists, not only summarize.
Similar memories can mislead; conflict detection is required.
Working-set selection should be tied to next action rather than full history.
applied_upgrades: Add conflict detection and visible drop_list steps.
Add misaligned memory replay failure mode.
sources: Agent memory survey / write-manage-read memory loop literature
Experience-following memory reliability research

source_basis: Frontend skill work should include state coverage, accessibility baseline, responsive behavior and API error handling.
Agent-generated UIs often miss non-happy-path states unless those states are explicit.
research_findings: Frontend skill needs explicit state matrix output.
Accessibility must be a gate, not later polish.
API error, permission and latency states belong in default edge-case set.
applied_upgrades: Add partial, latency and permission-denied edge cases.
Add data ownership and validation procedure emphasis.
remaining_risks: Needs browser-state snapshot evidence in Runner.
sources: WCAG/accessibility and frontend testing practice
Agent-generated UI state coverage risk

source_basis: Agent memory research shows naive memory growth can propagate errors and replay irrelevant experiences.
Memory surveys emphasize deletion, contradiction handling, latency budgets and privacy governance.
research_findings: Memory skill must include deletion/deprecation, not only addition.
Conflict handling is mandatory before durable storage.
Memory update packets need do_not_store and privacy-aware retention.
applied_upgrades: Add error propagation and memory growth failure modes.
remaining_risks: Needs policy for sensitive personal/project data classes.
sources: Agent memory survey / write-manage-read memory loop literature
